name: Title 1
text: Predicting conflict clusters in Africa
textPreview: Predicting conflict clusters in Africa

name: Subtitle 2
text:
A spatiotemporal ACLED pipeline
Data, hypotheses, and method design
June 2026
textPreview: A spatiotemporal ACLED pipeline | Data, hypotheses, and method design | June 2026

name: Title 1
text: Today’s focus
textPreview: Today’s focus

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
1. Why conflict clustering is a spatial question
2. What ACLED data contains
3. Turning events into a grid-cell monthly panel
4. Hypotheses: local persistence and spatial diffusion
5. Modeling and validation strategy
6. Reproducible pipeline and next steps
textPreview: 1. Why conflict clustering is a spatial question | 2. What ACLED data contains | 3. Turning events into a grid-cell monthly panel | 4. Hy...

title: The core idea: conflict risk moves through space and time

name: Title 1
text: The core idea: conflict risk moves through space and time
textPreview: The core idea: conflict risk moves through space and time

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
Instead of predicting “conflict in Africa” as one continent-wide number, the project treats conflict as a local and time-varying process.
Africa is divided into comparable geographic grid cells, observed month by month.
The key question is whether recent conflict in and around a cell helps predict conflict in that cell in the next month.
This is designed as an early-warning and risk-mapping exercise, not as a claim that the model has proven causality.
textPreview: Instead of predicting “conflict in Africa” as one continent-wide number, the project treats conflict as a local and time-varying process....

title: The ACLED data used in this first stage

name: Rubrik 1
text: The ACLED data used in this first stage
textPreview: The ACLED data used in this first stage

text:
Source: ACLED event data
Period: January 2000 to December 2010
Spatial scope: African countries in the file
Events: 41,299 reported events
Countries: 48
Core fields: date, coordinates, event type, actors, fatalities
Use here: input data for grid-cell monthly prediction
textPreview: Source: ACLED event data | Period: January 2000 to December 2010 | Spatial scope: African countries in the file | Events: 41,299 reported...

alt: Monthly count of ACLED events in the 2000 to 2010 data.

title: What one ACLED row tells us

name: Title 1
text: What one ACLED row tells us
textPreview: What one ACLED row tells us

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
Each row is a reported political violence or disorder event.
The row includes a date, latitude and longitude, country and administrative location, event and sub-event type, involved actors, fatalities, and source notes.
The raw row is not yet the unit of prediction. It is an observation that can be aggregated into a consistent space-time panel.
This matters because the model needs comparable units: the same places, observed repeatedly through time, including months with no reported conflict.
textPreview: Each row is a reported political violence or disorder event. | The row includes a date, latitude and longitude, country and administrativ...

name: Title 1
text: Research question and hypotheses
textPreview: Research question and hypotheses

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
Research question: do conflict clusters spread over time?
H1 Local persistence: a cell with recent conflict is more likely to experience conflict again in the following month.
H2 Spatial diffusion: conflict in nearby cells raises risk in the focal cell, even after accounting for the focal cell’s own history.
H3 Scale robustness: the same broad pattern should be visible when the grid is made smaller or larger.
textPreview: Research question: do conflict clusters spread over time? | H1 Local persistence: a cell with recent conflict is more likely to experienc...

title: From reported events to a prediction table

name: Title 1
text: From reported events to a prediction table
textPreview: From reported events to a prediction table

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
1. Start with point events: latitude, longitude, date, and event attributes.
2. Assign each event to an approximate 100 × 100 km grid cell.
3. Count events and fatalities by cell and month.
4. Add explicit rows for cell-months with zero reported events.
5. Shift the outcome forward: use earlier information to predict conflict next month.
textPreview: 1. Start with point events: latitude, longitude, date, and event attributes. | 2. Assign each event to an approximate 100 × 100 km grid c...

name: Rubrik 1
text: The 100 km grid-cell monthly panel
textPreview: The 100 km grid-cell monthly panel

text:
Grid: 100 × 100 km cells
Rows: 187,592 cell-months
Cells: 1,432 geographic cells
Months: 131 modeled months after target shift
Outcome: any conflict in the next month
Outcome frequency: about 8% positive cell-months
Purpose: a structured map-time table for prediction
textPreview: Grid: 100 × 100 km cells | Rows: 187,592 cell-months | Cells: 1,432 geographic cells | Months: 131 modeled months after target shift | Ou...

alt: Diagnostics for the 100 km grid-cell monthly panel.

title: What the model is allowed to know

name: Title 1
text: What the model is allowed to know
textPreview: What the model is allowed to know

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
Only information available before the prediction month is used.
Local history: whether the same cell had conflict in the last 1, 3, 6, or 12 months.
Intensity history: recent event counts and fatality counts, summarized over rolling windows.
Neighbor history: recent conflict in nearby grid cells, used to test spatial diffusion.
Context controls: month and country patterns, plus event-type histories where useful.
textPreview: Only information available before the prediction month is used. | Local history: whether the same cell had conflict in the last 1, 3, 6, ...

name: Title 1
text: Modeling strategy in plain language
textPreview: Modeling strategy in plain language

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
The models compare increasingly informative explanations of next-month conflict risk.
Baseline: broad place and time patterns.
Local-history model: adds what happened in the same cell recently.
Neighbor model: adds what happened around the cell recently.
Extended model: adds richer event-type and intensity information.
Logistic regression keeps the design interpretable; random forests test whether flexible patterns add predictive value.
textPreview: The models compare increasingly informative explanations of next-month conflict risk. | Baseline: broad place and time patterns. | Local-...

name: Title 1
text: How we avoid fooling ourselves
textPreview: How we avoid fooling ourselves

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
Time is split chronologically, not randomly, because the real task is forecasting future months.
Model development uses 2000-2005; validation uses 2006-2010.
The later 2011-2020 period is held back for a separate out-of-sample phase.
The main grid is 100 km × 100 km, with 50 km and 200 km robustness checks.
Evaluation should ask whether high-risk cells are useful and calibrated, not only whether a single threshold looks good.
textPreview: Time is split chronologically, not randomly, because the real task is forecasting future months. | Model development uses 2000-2005; vali...
